title: Fix the Simple Array Median Finder

statement.markdown: Write a JavaScript function that finds the median of a given array of integers. If the array is empty, return -1. If the array has an odd length, the median is the middle element. If the array has an even length, the median is the average of the two middle elements. For example, given the array [1, 3, 5], the function should return 3. Given the array [1, 2, 3, 4], the function should return 2.5.
statement.notes: The array will not be null, but it may be empty.

examples.explanation: The median of an empty array is -1.

hints.text: First hint text: Handle the edge case where the array is empty.
